title: platanus × acerifolia var. kelseyana
intro: Platanus × acerifolia var. kelseyana, also known as the London planetree, is a deciduous tree hybrid derived from the American and European plane trees. The bark and leaves of this tree have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The medicinal properties of Platanus × acerifolia var. kelseyana include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties, which contribute to its health benefits such as reducing swelling, promoting wound healing, and regulating blood pressure. The herbal preparations of this tree include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which can be used to make teas, salves, and creams for topical application.
